text: Agile methodology is a popular approach in software development. It focuses on iterative development, meaning the work is broken down into small cycles called sprints. Each sprint lasts a couple of weeks and delivers a working product feature. Every day, the team holds a quick standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. The project's to-do list is called a backlog, which contains user stories. These stories describe new features from the user's perspective. Throughout the project, the team regularly reviews and adjusts their plan based on feedback. This flexible system helps teams deliver value faster and respond to changes more easily.
